title: Redesign Add Metrics Bar with Dropdown Actions and Quick Entry
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing metrics, I want a compact 'Add Metric' button that reveals import options (Salesforce, Tableau, Bulk Import, Manual Entry) in a dropdown, so that I can quickly add metrics without a cluttered toolbar.
description:
Replace the current Add Metrics toolbar with a compact dropdown button based on extensive user feedback about space usage and discoverability.

## Design
1. **Add Metric button**: Clicking reveals a dropdown with CSS open animation
2. **Dropdown options** with gray icons and tooltip help:
   - Import from Salesforce (SVG icon)
   - Import from Tableau (SVG icon)
   - Bulk Import from Excel/CSV (existing icon)
   - Manual Entry (text field + Create Metric button)
3. **Visual design**: Card container, subtle hover highlight (`#f2fbf9`), dark mode divider color `#404b6f`, custom icon class `metricaddicon` (35px, font-weight 500)
4. **Manual entry UX**: Dropdown stays open after creating a metric so users can rapidly enter multiple metrics. Create Metric button hidden until user starts typing, with tooltip: "Click or press ENTER on your keyboard to create a metric."

## Dark Mode Support
- Dropdown border: `1px solid rgb(53, 82, 111)`
- Hover: `rgba(31, 197, 153, .15)`
- Divider: `#404b6f`
- Full dark mode styling for both light and dark themes
acceptance_criteria: Add Metric button opens a dropdown with CSS animation
Dropdown shows options for Salesforce, Tableau, Bulk Import, and Manual Entry with icons
Icons are gray (not green) to limit color to interactive elements only
Tooltip icons explain each import option
Hover highlight uses subtle color (#f2fbf9 in light mode)
Manual entry dropdown stays open after metric creation for rapid entry
Create Metric button is hidden until user starts typing
Create Metric button shows tooltip on hover
Pressing ENTER creates the metric without clicking
Dropdown renders correctly in both light and dark modes
All content in a single continuous window (no separate windows)
ux_changes: Replaced the full-width Add Metrics toolbar with a compact dropdown button. Dropdown includes Salesforce, Tableau, Bulk Import, and Manual Entry options with gray SVG icons. Manual entry mode keeps dropdown open for rapid multi-entry. Create Metric button hidden until typing begins. Full dark mode support.
implementation_notes: Used Bootstrap card container for the dropdown parent div. Custom CSS class `metricaddicon` for icon sizing (35px). Icons changed from H1 tags to custom class. Metric entry row gets 10px vertical padding via `.metricentry` class. Default text font size used instead of 14px override. Create Metric button initially hidden, appears active on keypress. Extensive CSS guidance provided by product owner for colors and spacing in both light and dark modes.
comment_summary: Iterative development with detailed CSS guidance from product owner. Key refinements: single continuous window instead of two, gray icons instead of green, subtle hover effect, manual entry keeps dropdown open for rapid entry, Create Metric button hidden until typing. Product owner provided specific CSS values for dark mode borders, hover, and dividers. Icons needed iteration to get correct SVGs for Salesforce and Tableau. Final design approved after multiple rounds of review.
